fact: February 17 is exactly 48 days into the year, which means there are 317 days left to go - that's almost 10 and a half months of fun still ahead!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for maple trees to start their sap flowing - the sweet stuff that becomes maple syrup drips fastest when days warm up but nights stay freezing cold!
February 17 falls smack in the middle of Aquarius season, the zodiac sign represented by a water bearer - even though it's actually an air sign, not a water sign!
In Antarctica, February 17 marks the height of summer, when temperatures can actually reach a balmy 50°F (10°C) - practically beach weather for penguins!
Daylight is growing by about 2-3 minutes per day in most northern places, meaning sunrise comes noticeably earlier than it did just a week ago!
February 17 sits exactly 4 weeks and 4 days before the spring equinox - close enough that some early spring flowers like crocuses might already be poking through snow in warmer spots!
In many cold regions, this date typically marks when ice on lakes and ponds starts making the loudest cracking and groaning sounds as temperature swings cause it to expand and contract!
Squirrels are often at their most frantic right now in northern climates, desperately digging through snow to find their buried acorn stashes before spring arrives!
The moon phase around February 17 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because winter air is clearer and less humid than summer air!
In the Southern Hemisphere, February 17 is typically peak harvest time for grapes, when they're bursting with the most sugar after months of hot summer sun!
